join_order.notes: Bitset ~250KB indexed by partkey-1. Parallel filter with schedule(static).
Pre-build flat array: struct{int32_t sk[4]; double cost[4];} per partkey. One cache line per lookup.
Pre-build uint8_t year_for_orderkey[max_orderkey+1]. Eliminates 240MB orders_orderkey_lookup, 57MB o_orderdate, and year_from_days in hot loop.

filters.method: memmem on varlen_string offsets_plus_data. PARALLEL with schedule(static) to minimize OpenMP overhead (200 dynamic chunks was causing 10ms overhead).

data_structures.supplier_nationkey: uint8_t[max_suppkey+1] — nationkey for each suppkey, direct index. suppkey 1-based contiguous so nationkey_for_supplier[sk] = s_nationkey[sk-1]. Use uint8_t since nationkey < 25.
data_structures.part_filter: bitset using uint64_t[(n_parts+63)/64]. partkeys are contiguous 1..n_parts. Parallel build with schedule(static). Use __atomic_or_fetch for thread-safe bit setting.
data_structures.year_for_orderkey: uint8_t[max_orderkey+1] dense array. Pre-built by scanning orders: for each order row r, year_for_orderkey[o_orderkey[r]] = year_from_days(o_orderdate[r]) - 1992. Requires mmap of orders/o_orderkey.bin (60MB, int32_t, 15M rows) and orders/o_orderdate.bin (60MB, int32_t, 15M rows). Build in parallel with schedule(static). Array size = 60M+1 bytes = 57MB. Access during main scan is semi-sequential since lineitem is sorted by l_orderkey. Eliminates orders_orderkey_lookup.bin (240MB) and year_from_days computation from hot loop.
data_structures.partsupp_compact: Flat array indexed by partkey: struct PSCompact { int32_t suppkey[4]; double supplycost[4]; } — 48 bytes per partkey. Array size = (max_partkey+1) * 48B = 2M * 48 = 91MB. Pre-built by scanning partsupp using partsupp_pk_index. For each partkey, read its partsupp entries (start, count from index), copy suppkeys and supplycosts into the compact struct. Build in parallel: partition partkeys across threads with schedule(static). During main scan: one cache line load gives all 4 (suppkey, supplycost) pairs for a partkey. Linear search 4 entries to find matching l_suppkey. Eliminates separate random access to ps_suppkey (30MB) and ps_supplycost (61MB) arrays.
data_structures.aggregation: thread-local double[25][8] direct 2D array (nation × year_offset). year_offset = year - 1992. Merge after parallel scan.

parallelism.notes: Phase ordering for I/O overlap: (1) mmap ALL files upfront at program start — all dimension files, all lineitem files, orders o_orderkey + o_orderdate, partsupp columns + index. Call prefetch() on lineitem columns immediately so OS readahead starts in background. (2) While readahead is running, do filter_part and pre-builds. (3) By main_scan time, lineitem pages are warm. filter_part: schedule(static) — uniform string lengths make static optimal, eliminates dynamic scheduling overhead (was 10ms, should be ~2ms). pre_build phases: schedule(static) over rows. main_scan: schedule(dynamic, 100000) for load balancing across qualifying rows. CRITICAL: do NOT call advise_random() on any column. All columns are either scanned sequentially during pre-build or accessed via pre-built structures during main scan.

indexes.purpose: Used during pre-build of partsupp_compact structure. Provides (start, count) for each partkey's entries in the partsupp table.

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category B+C. The 52ms unaccounted gap (53% of 98ms total) came from mmapping 2.4GB across 17 files between phases, with destructive madvise flips (MADV_SEQUENTIAL then MADV_RANDOM on 240MB orders_orderkey_lookup and 91MB partsupp columns). Fix: (1) Pre-build a dense year_for_orderkey[60M] uint8_t array by sequentially scanning o_orderkey + o_orderdate — eliminates the 240MB orders_orderkey_lookup.bin entirely plus the 57MB o_orderdate random access and per-row year_from_days computation; (2) Pre-build a compact partsupp_compact[2M] struct array (48B per partkey with 4 suppkeys+costs together) by sequentially scanning partsupp — replaces 3 separate random-access arrays (107MB) with 1 cache-line-aligned lookup; (3) Use schedule(static) for filter_part to eliminate OpenMP dynamic scheduling overhead (10ms → ~2ms); (4) mmap all files upfront and call prefetch() on lineitem columns to overlap OS readahead with pre-build computation. No madvise flip needed since all pre-build scans are sequential. Expected: mmap+prefetch(1ms) + filter(2ms) + prebuild(5ms) + main_scan(25ms) + output(2ms) = ~35ms.
